entries.applies_to: Any organization managing cybersecurity risk; voluntary but referenced across US federal guidance

entries.document: SP 800-53 Rev. 5 — Security and Privacy Controls for Information Systems and Organizations
entries.applies_to: US federal information systems; baseline control catalog underpinning FISMA and FedRAMP

entries.document: SP 800-171 Rev. 3 — Protecting Controlled Unclassified Information in Nonfederal Systems
entries.applies_to: Nonfederal systems and contractors handling Controlled Unclassified Information (CUI)
Organizations selecting, implementing, and authorizing security controls across a system lifecycle

entries.document: SP 800-30 Rev. 1 — Guide for Conducting Risk Assessments
entries.applies_to: Organizations conducting cybersecurity risk assessments at any organizational tier
Organizations designing zero-trust security architectures
Organizations managing enterprise privacy risk, as a companion to the Cybersecurity Framework
Organizations seeking certifiable information security management system requirements
Organizations implementing information security controls referenced by ISO/IEC 27001

entries.document: ISO/IEC 27005:2022 — Guidance on managing information security risks
entries.applies_to: Organizations managing information security risk within an ISMS
Organizations needing shared terminology and overview for the ISO/IEC 27000 family
Organizations of any size implementing prioritized cyber defense practices
Organizations securely configuring specific technology products and platforms

entries.document: Federal Information Security Modernization Act (FISMA) background
entries.applies_to: US federal agencies and their information systems
Cloud service providers seeking authorization to serve US federal government agencies
US federal civilian executive branch agencies, via binding operational and emergency directives
Organizations prioritizing remediation of actively exploited vulnerabilities

entries.document: Cybersecurity Risk Management, Strategy, Governance, and Incident Disclosure (Release 33-11216)
entries.applies_to: US public companies disclosing cybersecurity incidents and governance practices

entries.document: Executive Order 14028 — Improving the Nation's Cybersecurity
entries.applies_to: US federal agencies and their software supply chains
Operators of essential and important services in critical sectors across EU member states
Manufacturers and vendors of products with digital elements sold in the EU
Financial-sector entities and their ICT service providers operating in the EU
ENISA's mandate and the EU-wide cybersecurity certification scheme for products and services
EU-wide cybersecurity coordination, threat intelligence, and certification schemes
Service organizations reporting on security, availability, processing integrity, confidentiality, and privacy controls

entries.document: PCI DSS — Payment Card Industry Data Security Standard
entries.applies_to: Entities that store, process, or transmit payment cardholder data

entries.document: ISO/IEC 27017:2015 — Information security controls for cloud services
entries.applies_to: Cloud service providers and customers implementing cloud-specific security controls

entries.document: ISO/IEC 27018:2019 — Protection of PII in public clouds
entries.applies_to: Public cloud providers acting as processors of personally identifiable information

entries.document: CSA STAR — Security, Trust, Assurance and Risk registry
entries.applies_to: Cloud providers publishing security and privacy assurance self-assessments or certifications
Cloud providers and customers mapping security controls across cloud domains
